_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — Royal Champion final from L9.5 Royal-Grade to L10 Royal Champion. Apply 5 ADDITIVE Royal-Champion upgrades per Cavalry_Progression_Plan.md L10 entry + AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy (chased filigree, lion's-head emboss, per-lame gold piping, per-edge gilt trim, gold-trimmed crinet, embossed heraldic stamps all REMOVED — gold material change + volume + single-emblem flanchard cross + recolored caparison carry the upgrade).
_meta.input_image: image_urls[0] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L9_5/composite/cavalry_L9_5_v1_composited.png — placeholder keeper version, user will rewire to actual keeper at runtime. SINGLE-IMAGE INPUT — L1_Base scale anchor DROPPED at L10 to allow the new tall plumed crest vertical extent without compressing it back into the L1 framing envelope.
history: v1 (2026-05-07): initial author — L10 Royal Champion with 5 ADDITIVE adds per CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § K0 + AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy. (1) Tall plumed crest ADDED to kettle helm apex — central upward gold-fluted crest spike (~7 inches) rising from the kettle's domed crown between the L9 fins, topped with a tall red+yellow horsehair plume (~10 inches) arcing slightly back over the helm's tail. (2) Gilt finish ADDED to the steel lance head — full buttery-warm gold gilt finish covering the L7 steel leaf-tip lance head; gold end-caps at top and bottom of the wooden haft; pennon now red+yellow+gold (gold bordure added). NO chased filigree, NO per-flange-tip spiked finials. The wooden haft middle remains warm-brown wood. (3) Heavy pauldrons (4-lame) — the L8 3-lame pauldrons grow a fourth uppermost smaller cap on top. Plain steel, NO lion's-head or figural emboss — the 4th lame volume IS the upgrade. (4) Tassets flare wider and longer — the L8 2-wider-lame tassets extend further down (mid-thigh -> just-above-knee) and flare further outward at the bottom edge. Lames remain 2-per-tasset wider plates. NO per-lame gold edge piping. (5) Royal horse barding — gold-rosette boss centered on the L7 chamfron forehead between the existing forehead spike; flanchards from L9 each gain a single raised gold cross emblem matching the rider's cuirass cross (single emblem per flanchard, NOT per-edge piping); caparison RECOLORED from red+blue checkered to red+yellow heraldic with central gold cross emblem and a single gold-trim band along the lower edge; L9 crinet stays plain steel (NO gold-trim at every lame edge). Livery on rider GONE (tassets are now plate only). SINGLE-IMAGE INPUT — L1_Base anchor dropped to allow the new vertical extent (gold crest + tall plume) without compressing it back into L1 framing.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy (drift-corrected pass).
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v7 (2026-05-07): reframe L10-3 pauldron add to single-piece volume growth (was 4-stacked-lames with decorative rivet seam). Aligns with L6 + L8 single-piece reframe; consistent across the entire L4-L10 ramp.

v7 (2026-05-07): scrub stale "3-lame layered pauldrons" PRESERVE references — L4-L10 pauldron progression now reads as single continuous piece (L4 disc → L6 thicker dome → L8 dome + bicep extension → L10 dome+extension grown in volume). No stacked-plate construction language anywhere in the chain.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.
v9 (2026-05-07): residual chest-band PRESERVE/preamble references scrubbed.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

v8 (2026-05-07): caparison gold-trim band on lower edge is now PRESERVED from the prior-level input (no longer new at L10) since the trim band beat was pulled forward to L9.5. L10 horse barding now adds: chamfron gold-rosette + flanchard gold cross emblems + caparison panel recolor + central gold cross. The lower-edge gold-trim band carries through unchanged.

v9 (2026-05-07): drop the red+yellow horsehair plume from L10-1 helm beat per user feedback (kept the central gold crest spike — that's the gold decorative element the user wanted, with a fleur-de-lis flared tip). Helm at L10 = kettle base + neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement + helm gold trim band + half-visor + closed visor + backswept fins + central gold-fluted crest spike (NEW at L10). NO plume / horsehair / cloth above the helm. Framing extent reduced from 82-86% to 80-83% (no plume to accommodate).

v10 (2026-05-07): drop fleur-de-lis tip language from L10-1 helm beat per user feedback — the previous 'fleur-de-lis flared tip / 3-prong widening' description was producing a fleur-de-lis ornament in every generation. Spike now described as a clean smooth tapering cone to a single sharp pointed top, with explicit negatives locking out fleur-de-lis, lily, trefoil, 3-prong, and any heraldic-flower / flared / radial-decoration shape at the apex or along the spike's length.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible ADDITIVE Royal-Champion upgrades to the mounted cavalry knight in the input image, completing the rank ramp at this level Royal Champion. Every the levels above detail must remain visible at this level. The rider already wears the existing Royal-Grade kit: kettle helm with backswept neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement + buttery gold trim band at the brim line + half-visor + closed visor extending downward (face fully concealed) + backswept fins (now grown to ~7-8 inches at this level, more sharply curved) with gold-spiked fin caps at each fin's apex; steel bevor at the throat; full steel cuirass with medial ridge + 1 horizontal abdominal seam + buttery gold cross emblem (four-armed equilateral cross flat-inlaid onto the medial ridge at chest center); skirt band of yellow+red livery only (narrowed) — cuirass sits flush against the bevor (NO chest band of cloth above it); full-arm scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist; single-piece steel pauldrons on both shoulders (thicker domes + bicep coverage extension, no stacked-plate construction); steel vambraces on both forearms; steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered on top of bare-hand silhouettes (with leather grip cord still visible between gauntleted fingers and wooden haft); leather waist belt with plain leather belt and brass buckle; longer 2-wider-lame steel tassets at both hips covering mid-thigh; full steel sabatons on both boots; blue trousers; brown leather riding boots in stirrups; wooden spear haft (warm-brown wood) with all accumulated kit (iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings + reinforced socket) + steel leaf-tip head + red+yellow heraldic pennon with gold trim band along outer edges + gold tassel + thin gold ring at lance socket; hip pouch on spear-side hip (the only belt accessory). The horse already wears: full steel chamfron with forehead spike, plain-steel crinet on the back of the neck, plain-steel flanchards on both flanks, iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, iron peytral plate beneath the red+blue checkered caparison (central panel still visible between flanchards). THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — no white markings of any kind. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side. Do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement. (1) TALL GOLD CREST SPIKE ADDED TO THE KETTLE HELM APEX — between the existing backswept fins visible in the input image, ADD a single central upward GOLD-FLUTED CREST SPIKE rising vertically from the kettle's domed crown apex. The crest spike is approximately 7 inches tall, ~1.2 inches wide at its base, tapering smoothly along its full height to a single sharp pointed top (a clean conical spike with a smooth taper, NOT a fleur-de-lis, NOT a 3-prong tip, NOT any flared / lily / heraldic flower shape at the top). Shallow vertical flute grooves run the length of the spike body (4 to 6 flute grooves visible). RENDER THE SPIKE AS BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow contrasting strongly against the steel of the kettle helm and the steel of the existing fins — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base, (ii) soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow, (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. The crest spike rises BETWEEN the two existing backswept fins (the fins remain plain silver-gray steel matching the kettle), positioned at the centerline of the helm. NO horsehair plume is added at the apex of the crest spike — the gold-fluted spike itself is the helm's tall ornament, ending in its tip with no additional cloth, hair, or plume material above it. The helm reads visibly TALLER than at the prior level from this single beat alone: the new gold crest spike adds clear vertical extent above the existing fins. (2) FULL GILT FINISH ADDED TO THE STEEL LANCE HEAD + GOLD HAFT END-CAPS — keep the the levels above wooden spear haft EXACTLY (warm-brown wood with all its accumulated kit: iron pommel cap at the butt, leather grip cord wrap on the upper third, iron banding rings + reinforced socket below the leaf-tip — all preserved unchanged); keep the existing steel leaf-tip lance head shape and the pennon attachment point. ADD: (a) a FULL BUTTERY-WARM GOLD GILT FINISH covering the entire steel lance head (the leaf-tip surface previously neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) is now fully gilded buttery polished yellow gold — full material change to gold across the whole leaf-tip; NO chased filigree along the leaf edges, NO engraved patterns on the leaf face, NO per-edge gold piping — the gold material change IS the upgrade); (b) a small buttery polished yellow gold END-CAP at the very TOP of the wooden haft (a short cylindrical gold cap ~1.5 inches tall wrapping the haft just below the gilt lance head's socket); (c) a second small buttery polished yellow gold END-CAP at the very BOTTOM of the wooden haft (replacing the existing iron pommel cap finish — same shape, now polished gold instead of dark iron). The pennon attached just below the gilt lance head GAINS a thin gold BORDURE band along its three free edges (the existing red+yellow split panel is preserved; the new gold bordure is a thin gold border line running along the outer perimeter of the pennon's free edges, NOT a fill, NOT a recolor of the panels). Pennon now reads as red+yellow+gold at thumbnail. The wooden haft middle (between the existing iron banding rings and the existing leather grip cord wrap) REMAINS warm-brown wood — NO gold haft body, NO gold barrel inlay between the end-caps. (3) HEAVY PAULDRONS — VOLUME GROWTH (SINGLE-PIECE) — grow each single-piece pauldron (existing thicker dome + bicep coverage extension) further in volume. The pauldron's overall vertical extent grows by approximately 25%, its width by approximately 20%, and the dome's curve becomes more pronounced — reading as a single thicker, taller, broader piece of shaped steel covering shoulder + upper bicep. NOT a stacked-plate construction; NOT a separate fourth cap added on top of the existing piece; NOT a hinged lame riveted into place — the existing pauldron reads as the SAME single continuous piece of shaped steel as, simply more voluminous. Both pauldrons grow symmetrically. Pauldrons visibly broader and taller at the shoulder line than (silhouette mightiness rule — peak shoulder width at this level). RENDER THE PAULDRON AS PLAIN COOL SILVER-GRAY STEEL — NO sculpted lion's-head motif, NO figural beast emboss, NO heraldic crest motif, NO gold trim, NO per-edge piping, NO surface engraving, NO decorative rivet rows on any pauldron seam. The volume bump IS the upgrade; the surface stays plain steel matching the the levels above pauldron material. (4) TASSETS FLARE WIDER AND LONGER — the 2-wider-lame tassets at each hip extend further DOWNWARD (the lower edge of each tasset moves from mid-thigh to just-above-knee, ~30% longer than the tassets visible in the input image) and FLARE FURTHER OUTWARD at the bottom edge (the lower edge of each tasset angles outward roughly 15 degrees from vertical, ~5-7 degrees more outward than the existing baseline flare). Lames REMAIN 2-per-tasset wider plates — NO return to thin multi-lame fragmentation, NO splitting into 3+ narrow lames. RENDER THE TASSET LAMES AS PLAIN COOL SILVER-GRAY STEEL — NO per-lame gold edge piping along each lame's perimeter, NO gold trim band at the bottom edge, NO gold inlay scrollwork, NO gold rivet caps. The volume + outward flare carry the upgrade. The yellow+red livery skirt band that was visible between the cuirass bottom and the existing tasset top is now COVERED — at the livery on the rider is GONE (tassets are now plate only against the cuirass, no livery showing between them). (5) ROYAL HORSE BARDING — the horse gains four coordinated additions: (a) a GOLD-ROSETTE BOSS centered on the existing chamfron's forehead BETWEEN the eyes and BELOW the existing forehead spike — a small circular buttery polished yellow gold rosette (a flat round gold disc ~2 inches diameter with shallow petal-like radial grooves) flush on the chamfron's steel surface; the existing forehead spike above it remains neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) unchanged; (b) on EACH flanchard (the existing plain-steel side plates on both flanks), ADD a SINGLE RAISED GOLD CROSS EMBLEM centered on the flanchard's outer face — a four-armed equilateral cross of buttery polished yellow gold, each arm ~3 inches long and ~1 inch wide, raised slightly proud of the steel surface (NOT flat inlay), reading as the same cross shape as the rider's cuirass cross. ONE emblem per flanchard, TWO total. NO per-edge gold piping on the flanchards; NO additional gold ornament beyond the single cross emblem; NO heraldic-stamp surface ornament; (c) the CAPARISON RECOLORED from the the levels above red+blue checkered pattern + steel-blue trim band to a NEW RED+YELLOW HERALDIC pattern: the caparison body is now divided into broad warm crimson-red and mustard-yellow heraldic panels (echoing the rider's surcoat livery palette, NOT the original blue), with a CENTRAL GOLD CROSS EMBLEM matching the cuirass and flanchard crosses (a single large four-armed gold cross prominently centered on the caparison's flank panel visible to camera) and a SINGLE GOLD-TRIM BAND along the lower edge of the caparison (a clean gold strip ~1 inch wide running along the bottom hem); (d) the existing STEEL CRINET on the horse's neck REMAINS PLAIN COOL SILVER-GRAY STEEL — NO per-lame gold trim, NO gold edge piping at every lame's seam, NO gold rivets. The crinet's silver-gray steel material is preserved unchanged from. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: NOT VISIBLE — fully concealed by the existing closed visor (preserved at this level). NO skin, NO hair, NO eyes, NO mustache visible above the bevor. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the the levels above kettle helm with all accumulated additions (backswept neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement, buttery gold trim band at the brim line, half-visor, closed visor extending downward to fully conceal the face, backswept fins ~7-8 inches tall with gold spike caps at each fin's apex — UNCHANGED at this level; the new plumed gold crest sits BETWEEN the existing fins, NOT replacing them). THE RIDER NECK: the existing steel bevor / collar plate at the throat, unchanged. THE RIDER TORSO: the existing reinforced cuirass with medial ridge + 1 horizontal abdominal seam + gold cross emblem at chest center on the medial ridge — UNCHANGED at this level (the cuirass cross is the same one introduced at this level; do NOT enlarge, raise, or recolor it; do NOT add additional gold ornament to the cuirass surface beyond the existing cross). The cuirass continues to sit FLUSH against the bevor — NO chest band of cloth above the cuirass at this level (chest band was removed at this level). The skirt band of yellow+red livery between cuirass bottom and tasset top is now COVERED at this level (livery on rider GONE per Edit Goal item 4). THE WIDE BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT: brass buckle (plain leather, no studs), unchanged. THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the EXISTING FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist (unchanged); the EXISTING single-piece steel pauldrons on both shoulders (disc → thicker dome → dome + bicep coverage extension, single continuous piece, no stacked plates) with the new 4th uppermost lame ADDED on top per Edit Goal item 3; the EXISTING steel vambraces on the forearms; the EXISTING steel finger-plates + CLEAN knuckle disc layered on top of bare-hand silhouettes; the existing leather grip cord still visible between gauntleted fingers and the wooden haft; both hands gripping the wooden spear haft and the brown leather reins. THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue trousers visible at the calf below the existing full sabatons; chunky warm-brown leather riding boots — body still visible at calf and heel above sabaton; the EXISTING full steel sabatons on both boots; both boots in stirrups; EXISTING longer 2-wider-lame steel tassets at both hips, EXTENDED FURTHER DOWN and FLARED FURTHER OUTWARD per Edit Goal item 4. THE WEAPON: the wooden spear haft warm-brown wood (the levels above unchanged in the middle section) with the existing iron pommel cap and leather grip cord and iron banding rings + reinforced socket all preserved; the existing steel leaf-tip lance head shape preserved but now FULLY GILDED buttery polished yellow gold per Edit Goal item 2; gold haft end-caps added at top and bottom of haft per Edit Goal item 2; the existing red+yellow heraldic pennon preserved with gold trim band + gold tassel + thin gold ring at lance socket, plus the new thin gold BORDURE band along the pennon's free edges per Edit Goal item 2. THE REINS: the brown leather reins at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed by the gauntleted rein-side hand. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT — head and muzzle on the rein-side, rump and tail on the spear-side, four-square stance, neck arched, ears forward. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Mane visible as a thin line between the existing plain-steel crinet's lames; tail dark brown extending behind the rump. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with iron banding on pommel and cantle; the iron-buckled bridle with iron browband; the existing full steel chamfron with forehead spike — preserved unchanged at except for the new gold-rosette boss added per Edit Goal item 5(a); the existing plain-steel crinet on the neck — UNCHANGED, NO per-lame gold trim per Edit Goal item 5(d); the existing plain-steel flanchards on both flanks — preserved unchanged except for the new single raised gold cross emblem on each flanchard per Edit Goal item 5(b); the existing iron peytral plate beneath the caparison. THE CAPARISON: RECOLORED at per Edit Goal item 5(c) — broad red+yellow heraldic panels with central gold cross emblem and single gold-trim band along the lower edge. NO checkered pattern at this level. NO blue trim band at this level. The caparison's silhouette, drape, lower edge tabbed shape, and position all remain identical to the existing input — only the surface palette + central emblem + lower trim are changed. THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: ALL preserved unchanged in shape and position (palette changes only as enumerated in the Edit Goals — caparison recolor and lance head gilt). NO knife sheath at any hip (removed at). The hip pouch remains as the only belt accessory. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately 15-25 degrees toward the viewer's left. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the gauntleted hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing leather grip cord wrap. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down; the gauntleted hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers. The pose reads as a calm royal-champion mounted patrol stance, with a clearly mightier silhouette than — taller helm via plumed gold crest + tall plume, broader pauldrons via 4th lame, longer flaring tassets, fully gilt lance head, royal horse barding (gold cross flanchards + recolored heraldic caparison + chamfron rosette). NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather, distinct buttery gold material reading clearly distinct from steel at thumbnail. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE — L1_BASE ANCHOR DROPPED at this level ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 82-86% of canvas vertical height — the new tall plumed gold crest + 10-inch horsehair plume push the rider's vertical extent meaningfully ABOVE the existing fin height. The wooden spear haft + gilt lance head also extend UP past the helm's plume tip on the spear-side, with the gilt leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. Headroom above the gold crest spike tip approximately 6-9% of canvas height — keep the crest spike tip + lance leaf-tip clearly INSIDE the canvas, NOT cropped at the top edge. BOTTOM of horse hooves at approximately 8-10% from bottom of canvas. Horizontal envelope from horse rump on the spear-side to horse muzzle on the rein-side approximately 60-62% of canvas width (unchanged from.5). Rider+horse vertically centered with the upward growth absorbed at the top via the smaller headroom. DO NOT zoom in (the additional vertical extent (gold crest spike) must be accommodated by reduced headroom, NOT by shrinking the rider+horse silhouette); DO NOT shift the figure to the bottom edge; DO NOT crop the gold crest spike tip, helm fins, gilt lance leaf-tip, or hooves; DO NOT add wider borders. Clean opaque WHITE background — no light gray tint, no off-white shading, no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT add chased filigree along the gilt lance head's leaf edges, engraved scrollwork on the lance face, or per-edge gold piping on the lance head — the gold material change IS the upgrade; DO NOT add per-flange-tip spiked finials, sculpted spikes, or pointed gold appendages on the gilt lance head; DO NOT replace the wooden spear haft middle with a gold haft body — only the TOP and BOTTOM end-caps are gold, the haft middle stays warm-brown wood; DO NOT add a lion's-head emboss, beast-head motif, figural emboss, or heraldic crest on the new 4th pauldron lame — it is PLAIN neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed); DO NOT add gold trim, gold edge piping, or gold rivets on the 4th pauldron lame or any pauldron lame; DO NOT add per-lame gold edge piping along each tasset lame's perimeter — the tassets stay PLAIN steel except the gold cross context on the cuirass; DO NOT add gold inlay scrollwork or gold rivet caps on the tassets; DO NOT split the tassets into 3+ narrow lames — they remain 2-per-tasset wider plates; DO NOT add a gold-trim band at the bottom edge of the tassets; DO NOT add gold gauntlet knuckle-plates, raised gold knuckle-discs, per-knuckle gold caps, or any gold decoration on the gauntlets at this level — the gauntlet knuckle disc remains CLEAN steel; DO NOT add gold-trim per-lame piping at every crinet lame edge — the crinet stays PLAIN steel; DO NOT add embossed studs, gold studs, or rivet-pattern decorations on the chamfron, saddle, belt, or shield rim; DO NOT stamp or emboss a heraldic shape on the flanchards beyond the SINGLE gold cross emblem per Edit Goal item 5(b); DO NOT add a second emblem, multiple emblems, or a row of crosses on each flanchard — exactly ONE gold cross per flanchard; DO NOT add gold filigree, scrollwork, chased patterns, engraved motifs, or sculpted figural emboss to ANY surface at this level; DO NOT replace or remove the existing backswept fins, the existing fin gold spike caps, the existing cuirass gold cross emblem, the existing closed visor, the existing half-visor, the existing helm gold trim band, or the existing backswept neck-guard skirt — all preserved unchanged underneath the new plumed crest; DO NOT replace the kettle helm with a sallet, closed great helm, or any non-kettle helm shape — the kettle base stays at with all accumulated additions; DO NOT show the rider's face at this level — fully concealed by the existing closed visor; DO NOT replace the wooden spear haft middle with a steel haft — only the lance HEAD is gilt; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory; DO NOT add additional pouches beyond the existing hip pouch on the spear-side hip; DO NOT change the horse coat color from solid chestnut brown; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT remove the existing plain-steel crinet, plain-steel flanchards (only the single gold cross emblem is added per flanchard), chamfron (only the gold-rosette boss is added on the forehead between the eyes), or iron peytral; DO NOT keep the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern at this level — it MUST recolor to red+yellow heraldic with central gold cross + single gold-trim band per Edit Goal item 5(c); DO NOT add gold piping along every panel seam of the recolored caparison — only the single central gold cross + single gold-trim band along the lower edge;
